root_text.sn32.13-52:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn32.13-52:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā uṇhavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn32.13-52:1.7: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena sucaritaṁ carati, vācāya sucaritaṁ carati, manasā sucaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn32.13-52:1.11: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā vassavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn32.13-52:1.14: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā vassavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn32.13-52:1.15: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā vassavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn32.13-52:0.3: How Giving Helps to Become a Warm Thundercloud God, Etc.
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the gods of warm thunderclouds …
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.7: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does good things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.9: ‘The gods of rainy thunderclouds are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.11: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the gods of rainy thunderclouds!’
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.14: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the gods of rainy thunderclouds.
translation_text.sn32.13-52:1.15: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the gods of rainy thunderclouds.”
